column1: Why does torch.version.cuda (12.4) differ from nvidia-smi CUDA (13.1)?
column2: nvidia-smi shows the max CUDA version the DRIVER supports (backward compatible). torch.version.cuda shows the CUDA RUNTIME bundled with the PyTorch wheel. They are independent — you do NOT need to install CUDA toolkit separately.

Overrides the default PyPI index. pip fetches packages ONLY from that URL. Used for PyTorch CUDA channels, private corp mirrors (NVIDIA NGC, HF), etc. Use --extra-index-url if you want PyPI as a fallback.

column1: How much VRAM does full fine-tuning a 7B model need vs QLoRA?
column2: Full fine-tune: ~80+ GB (multi-GPU). QLoRA (4-bit via bitsandbytes + peft): ~6–8 GB. On a 4 GB T1000 → fine-tune 1B–3B locally; 7B goes to free Colab/Kaggle T4/P100.
